title: Use TimeZoneInfo.FindSystemTimeZoneById without converting the timezones with TimezoneConverter
description: Use "TimeZoneInfo.FindSystemTimeZoneById" without converting the timezones with "TimezoneConverter"Analyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellSince .NET 6 you don’t have to use theTimeZoneConverterlibrary to manually do the conversion between IANA and Windows timezones. The .NET 6.0 introduced new Time Zone enhancements, one being theTimeZoneInfo.FindSystemTimeZoneById(string timezone)method now accepts as input both IANA and Windows time zone IDs on any operating system with installed time zone data.TimeZoneInfo.FindSystemTimeZoneByIdwill automatically convert its input from IANA to Windows and vice versa if the requested time zone is not found on the system.Why is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoThe methodTimeZoneInfo.FindSystemTimeZoneById(string timezone)can
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
using TimeZoneConverter;
var tz = TZConvert.GetTimeZoneInfo("Eastern Standard Time");

checks.message: Use TimeZoneInfo.FindSystemTimeZoneById instead of TimeZoneConverter.TZConvert - .NET 6+ handles IANA/Windows timezone conversion automatically

logic.detection_conditions: Call to TZConvert.GetTimeZoneInfo from TimeZoneConverter library
logic.reasoning: Since .NET 6, TimeZoneInfo.FindSystemTimeZoneById automatically handles conversion between IANA and Windows timezone IDs. The TimeZoneConverter library is no longer needed and adds unnecessary dependency. Using the built-in method is cleaner and has better performance.
